nodes.x: Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul.
Wherefore, the days will come that no flesh shall be safe upon the waters.

Behold and hearken unto the voice of him who has all power, who is from everlasting to everlasting, even Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. Behold, verily thus saith the Lord unto you, O ye elders of my church, who are assembled upon this spot, whose sins are now forgiven you, for I the Lord forgive sins, and am merciful unto those who confess their sins with humble hearts ; but verily I…
For this shall every one that is godly pray unto thee in a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they shall not come nigh unto him.

waters cover the sea. But woe be to those who reject the gospel, and refuse to comply with all the requisitions of heaven; dismay, horror of a guilty conscience, and finally, banishment from the presence of God will be their fate.
I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing: I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink: let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
That saith to the deep, Be dry, and I will dry up thy rivers:
I have digged and drunk strange waters, and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of besieged places.
When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.
I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.
My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where is thy God?
My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored that which I took not away.
Consider mine enemies; for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
I hate them with perfect hatred: I count them mine enemies.
O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.
Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin—; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.
Yet thou sayest, Because I am innocent, surely his anger shall turn from me. Behold, I will plead with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned.

Behold thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the Lord, and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that he that findeth me will slay me, because of mine iniquities, for these things are not hid from the Lord.
Let not them that wait on thee, O Lord God of hosts, be ashamed for my sake: let not those that seek thee be confounded for my sake, O God of Israel.
Thou therefore, O Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.

And I will not suffer, saith the Lord of Hosts, that the cries of the fair daughters of this people, which I have led out of the land of Jerusalem, shall come up unto me against the men of my people, saith the Lord of Hosts.
Thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, Amend your ways and your doings, and I will cause you to dwell in this place.
Thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, unto all that are carried away captives, whom I have caused to be carried away from Jerusalem unto Babylon;

For the Lord, the God of Israel, saith that he hateth putting away: for one covereth violence with his garment, saith the Lord of hosts: therefore take heed to your spirit, that ye deal not treacherously.
Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.
We are confounded, because we have heard reproach: shame hath covered our faces: for strangers are come into the sanctuaries of the Lord’s house.
Lord, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?
My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,
Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproach among men.

Because thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth, but hast fretted me in all these things; behold, therefore I also will recompense thy way upon thine head, saith the Lord God: and thou shalt not commit this lewdness above all thine abominations.
I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother’s children.
And strangers shall stand and feed your flocks, and the sons of the alien shall be your plowmen and your vinedressers.
They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.
Wherefore, Father, spare these my brethren that believe on my name, that they may come unto me and have everlasting life.

Or in other words, yield yourselves up unto us, and unite with us and become acquainted with our secret works, and become our brethren that ye may be like unto us—not our slaves, but our brethren and partners of all our substance.
As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
For the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.
And his disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.
And the man of thine, whom I shall not cut off from mine altar, shall be to consume thine eyes, and to grieve thine heart: and all the increase of thine house shall die in the flower of their age.
My zeal hath consumed me, because mine enemies have forgotten thy words.
Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;
Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
When I wept, and chastened my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.

And my soul hungered; and I kneeled down before my Maker, and I cried unto him in mighty prayer and supplication for mine own soul; and all the day long did I cry unto him; yea, and when the night came I did still raise my voice high that it reached the heavens.
18 Two years therefore I afflicted my soul with fasting in the fear of the Lord, and I learnt that deliverance from envy cometh by the fear of God.

And it came to pass that I was three days and three nights in the most bitter pain and anguish of soul; and never, until I did cry out unto the Lord Jesus Christ for mercy, did I receive a remission of my sins. But behold, I did cry unto him and I did find peace to my soul.
My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.
¶ And it came to pass, when I heard these words, that I sat down and wept, and mourned certain days, and fasted, and prayed before the God of heaven,
I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and I make sackcloth their covering.
But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting; and my prayer returned into mine own bosom.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.

till the earth and reap a crop, we produced wheat and corn and potatoes. We turned our cattle on to the range to make our beef. We had plenty of wheat. We began to make our clothing here. We drove in sheep and we took care of the wool, and made it into cloth. I brought a carding machine with me. It was the only one in the Territory for years, and it carded up a great deal of wool. We made up this…
I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.
They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I was the song of the drunkards.
And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.
And now, I desire to know the cause whereby ye were so bold as to come near the walls of the city, when I, myself, was with my guards without the gate?
And also because I said unto them that they were a lost and a fallen people they were angry with me, and sought to lay their hands upon me, that they might cast me into prison.
And I set before the sons of the house of the Rechabites pots full of wine, and cups, and I said unto them, Drink ye wine.
But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, O Lord, in an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.
Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.
Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink: let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing: I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
Send thine hand from above; rid me, and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of strange children;
Rid me, and deliver me from the hand of strange children, whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood:
Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
Let not the waterflood overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit shut her mouth upon me.
Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass, that when the virgin cometh forth to draw water, and I say to her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water of thy pitcher to drink;
How long wilt thou not depart from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?
Waters flowed over mine head; then I said, I am cut off.
Hear me, O Lord; for thy lovingkindness is good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
Let thy tender mercies come unto me, that I may live: for thy law is my delight.
Let thy mercies come also unto me, O Lord, even thy salvation, according to thy word.
Great are thy tender mercies, O Lord: quicken me according to thy judgments.
Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O Lord: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble: hear me speedily.
Hide not thy face from me in the day when I am in trouble; incline thine ear unto me: in the day when I call answer me speedily.
Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; and teach me thy statutes.
Deal bountifully with thy servant, that I may live, and keep thy word.
Hide not thy face far from me; put not thy servant away in anger: thou hast been my help; leave me not, neither forsake me, O God of my salvation.
Hear me speedily, O Lord: my spirit faileth: hide not thy face from me, lest I be like unto them that go down into the pit.
Draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it: deliver me because of mine enemies.
For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.

Therefore this is thy gift; apply unto it, and blessed art thou, for it shall deliver you out of the hands of your enemies, when, if it were not so, they would slay you and bring your soul to destruction.

4. Therefore this is thy gift, apply unto it, and blessed art
    thou, for it shall deliver you out of the hands of your enemies,
    when, if it were not so they would slay you and bring your soul to
    destruction.
Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?
Thou hast known my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries are all before thee.
O my God, I trust in thee: let me not be ashamed, let not mine enemies triumph over me.

Thou art become guilty in thy blood that thou hast shed; and hast defiled thyself in thine idols which thou hast made; and thou hast caused thy days to draw near, and art come even unto thy years: therefore have I made thee a reproach unto the heathen, and a mocking to all countries.
¶ O Lord, thou knowest: remember me, and visit me, and revenge me of my persecutors; take me not away in thy longsuffering: know that for thy sake I have suffered rebuke.
I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
That thou mayest bear thine own shame, and mayest be confounded in all that thou hast done, in that thou art a comfort unto them.
Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: and I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; and for comforters, but I found none.

My heart hath been weighed down with sorrow from time to time, for I have feared, lest for the hardness of your hearts the Lord your God should come out in the fulness of his wrath upon you, that ye be cut off and destroyed forever;
Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad.
I am feeble and sore broken: I have roared by reason of the disquietness of my heart.
The troubles of my heart are enlarged: O bring thou me out of my distresses.

Now when our hearts were depressed, and we were about to turn back, behold, the Lord comforted us, and said: Go amongst thy brethren, the Lamanites, and bear with patience thine afflictions, and I will give unto you success.
They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
¶ They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.
Which did eat the fat of their sacrifices, and drank the wine of their drink offerings? let them rise up and help you, and be your protection.
And I have consumed them, and wounded them, that they could not arise: yea, they are fallen under my feet.
And it came to pass that I did slay wild beasts, insomuch that I did obtain food for our families.
And it came to pass that they did come unto me and partake of the fruit also.
Let their table become a snare before them: and that which should have been for their welfare, let it become a trap.
And David saith, Let their table be made a snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a recompence unto them:
Four tables were on this side, and four tables on that side, by the side of the gate; eight tables, whereupon they slew their sacrifices.
Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.
Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.
Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.
Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
And let them make haste, and take up a wailing for us, that our eyes may run down with tears, and our eyelids gush out with waters.
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
And the eyes of them that see shall not be dim, and the ears of them that hear shall hearken.
Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.
Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful anger take hold of them.
Now, O Lord, because of this their humility wilt thou turn away thine anger, and let thine anger be appeased in the destruction of those wicked men whom thou hast already destroyed.
Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
Let thine anger be kindled against our enemies; and, in the fury of thine heart, with thy sword avenge us of our wrongs.
This we will do to them; we will even let them live, lest wrath be upon us, because of the oath which we sware unto them.
Now will I shortly pour out my fury upon thee, and accomplish mine anger upon thee: and I will judge thee according to thy ways, and will recompense thee for all thine abominations.
Let their habitation be desolate; and let none dwell in their tents.
Notwithstanding the land shall be desolate because of them that dwell therein, for the fruit of their doings.
And they shall be desolate in the midst of the countries that are desolate, and her cities shall be in the midst of the cities that are wasted.
And what the land is that they dwell in, whether it be good or bad; and what cities they be that they dwell in, whether in tents, or in strong holds;
And they shall do like unto the children of Israel, pitching their tents by the way.
So will I stretch out my hand upon them, and make the land desolate, yea, more desolate than the wilderness toward Diblath, in all their habitations: and they shall know that I am the Lord.
For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.
And if they shall smite this people thou wilt smite them; thou wilt fight for thy people as thou didst in the day of battle, that they may be delivered from the hands of all their enemies.
And they spake unto him, saying, If thou be kind to this people, and please them, and speak good words to them, they will be thy servants for ever.
Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.
And Amulon commanded them that they should stop their cries; and he put guards over them to watch them, that whosoever should be found calling upon God should be put to death.

Therefore thou deliveredst them into the hand of their enemies, who vexed them: and in the time of their trouble, when they cried unto thee, thou heardest them from heaven; and according to thy manifold mercies thou gavest them saviours, who saved them out of the hand of their enemies.
Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.
And thus saith the Lord God: I will cause that they shall be loathsome unto thy people, save they shall repent of their iniquities.
That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.
And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the Lord our God shall cut them off.
And if he or she do any manner of iniquity, he or she shall be delivered up unto the law, even that of God.
Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
Let them be before the Lord continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.

And let them journey from thence preaching the word by the way, saying none other things than that which the prophets and apostles have written, and that which is taught them by the Comforter through the prayer of faith.
And let them also return, preaching the gospel by the way, bearing record of the things which are revealed unto them.
But I am poor and sorrowful: let thy salvation, O God, set me up on high.
For I will raise up unto myself a pure people, that will serve me in righteousness;
But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
Praise ye the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.
I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call upon the name of the Lord.
In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.
thanksgiving and joy, to the Lord. I pray that the remainder of my days may pass in His service, in the building up of His kingdom."
This also shall please the Lord better than an ox or bullock that hath horns and hoofs.

And he shall go out unto the altar that is before the Lord, and make an atonement for it; and shall take of the blood of the bullock, and of the blood of the goat, and put it upon the horns of the altar round about.
For thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.
Our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not an hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the Lord our God; and we know not with what we must serve the Lord, until we come thither.
Also a bullock and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before the Lord; and a meat offering mingled with oil: for to day the Lord will appear unto you.
And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the Lord: and the bullock shall be killed before the Lord.
The humble shall see this, and be glad: and your heart shall live that seek God.
And it is my will that you shall humble yourselves before me, and obtain this blessing by your diligence and humility and the prayer of faith.
Be thou humble; and the Lord thy God shall lead thee by the hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers.
Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.

humble and diligent, and the good Spirit of the Lord will attend you from this hour, and you will finally be saved in our Father's kingdom; which I earnestly pray may be the happy lot of you and all good Saints, in the name of Jesus Christ our Savior. Amen.
For the Lord heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners.
Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, because the Lord is his refuge.
That thou mayest say to the prisoners, Go forth; to them that are in darkness, Shew yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their pastures shall be in all high places.
He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.
Let the heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moveth therein.
Praise him, ye heavens of heavens, and ye waters that be above the heavens.
And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the praise of the world.
And give him no rest, till he establish, and till he make Jerusalem a praise in the earth.
And worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters—
Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.
For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah: that they may dwell there, and have it in possession.

return back to Palestine in Asia and rebuild their city of Jerusalem and a temple within that city before, and preparatory to the coming of the Lord. Ezekiel, in describing the latter-day building of Jerusalem, says, “And the name of the city from that day forward shall be ‘The Lord is there.’” After the rebuilding of that city it will never be forsaken, or plucked up. As Jeremiah says in his…

Therefore he said unto Judah, Let us build these cities, and make about them walls, and towers, gates, and bars, while the land is yet before us; because we have sought the Lord our God, we have sought him, and he hath given us rest on every side. So they built and prospered.
Therefore shall Zion for your sake be plowed as a field, and Jerusalem shall become heaps, and the mountain of the house as the high places of the forest.

We shall in due time walk forth into Jackson County and build up the waste places of Zion. We shall erect in that county a beautiful city after the order and pattern that the Lord shall reveal, part of which has already been revealed. God intends to have a city built up that will never be destroyed nor overcome, but that will exist while eternity shall endure; and he will point out the pattern…
The seed also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his name shall dwell therein.
His soul shall dwell at ease; and his seed shall inherit the earth.
A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.
Only the Lord had a delight in thy fathers to love them, and he chose their seed after them, even you above all people, as it is this day.

Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.
The children of thy servants shall continue, and their seed shall be established before thee.
